retrieval_policy.stable_claim_priority: 主题库/ first, then governance rules, then generated review context.
retrieval_policy.workbench_default: cautious or exclude unless the user asks for process context.
retrieval_policy.citation_rule: When citing cautious material, include path and review or promotion status.

items.reason: Formal knowledge cards, decision records, and topic indexes live here after review and promotion.
conditions: Prefer files with type knowledge-card, decision-record, or topic-index.
Use source_sessions and status fields when citing claims.

items.reason: Machine-readable status, review context, dashboard, and tool boundary snapshots.
conditions: Refresh with tools/kb refresh-index after accepted writes.
Treat generated-agent-brief, generated-promote-audit, and generated-version-status as runtime-local snapshots.

items.path: _meta/知识入库规则.md, _meta/工作流说明.md, _meta/版本管理规则.md, _meta/Knowledge Workbench V0 实施计划.md
items.reason: Defines admission, workflow, versioning, and current implementation goals.
conditions: Use these rules to interpret workbench state before proposing writes.
Do not override explicit human review gates.

items.reason: Expert personas and durable viewpoints used to structure multi-expert discussions.
conditions: Use as role context, not as factual evidence.
Separate expert stance from confirmed knowledge claims.

items.reason: Adapter plans for Obsidian, Khoj, MCP, and input workflows.
conditions: Use for integration behavior and UI entry points.
Do not treat draft integration plans as completed implementation.

items.reason: Condensed candidate knowledge before formal promotion.
conditions: Only cite with promotion_status and path.
Pending-human-review content can inform discussion but is not formal knowledge.
Approved-for-promotion still requires promote-audit and promote --confirm before entering 主题库/.

items.reason: Red-blue debate traces and multi-expert reasoning history.
conditions: Use for process context, objections, and unresolved assumptions.
Do not answer stable factual questions from debate text alone.
Use to reconstruct context and source_sessions.
Treat claims as provisional unless promoted or reviewed elsewhere.

items.reason: Raw or semi-processed source notes may contain unverified, clipped, or placeholder material.
conditions: Use only when explicitly reviewing a source note.
Do not include by default in broad RAG answers.
Promote source value through review-source or distillation workflows, not direct retrieval priority.

items.reason: Templates, local runtime state, Git internals, and media are not stable knowledge sources.
conditions: Only inspect when debugging a template, Obsidian setup, Git issue, or visual asset.
Never use these paths as formal answer evidence.

recommended_query_order.label: Formal knowledge and rules
recommended_query_order.use_for: Stable claims, decisions, admission rules, workflow, and versioning.

recommended_query_order.label: Generated machine context
recommended_query_order.use_for: Current state, queues, human decision context, and safe tool boundaries.

recommended_query_order.label: Expert and integration context
recommended_query_order.use_for: Expert perspectives, adapter behavior, and UI or client integration plans.

recommended_query_order.label: Workbench process context
recommended_query_order.use_for: Reasoning traces, provisional candidates, objections, and source reconstruction.

guardrails: Retrieval scope does not equal formal knowledge promotion.
Prefer 主题库/ for stable claims and cite source_sessions when available.
Use _workbench/ material as process context unless a human explicitly asks to inspect it.
Never treat _workbench/inbox/ as default RAG evidence.
Generated runtime snapshots should be refreshed before cross-client handoff.
Do not call review-source, review-distillation, approve-distillation, or promote --confirm from retrieval results alone.
